persons.arguments_against: Single-initial bio (George). Assigned only the Leeward Islands governorship, which matches the entry ('governor, Leeward Islands, Oct. 1874') verbatim with a 100 position score and the shared C.M.G.(1874) — an independently strong link despite the bare forename. Surname OCR-garbled BERKLEY~Berkeley.
persons.would_overturn: A second same-surname person serving the same colony in the same years and offices, or a printed birth/death date excluding these years.

unassigned_stints.reason: L. G. A. Beckley, Assistant Treasurer, Sierra Leone 1950 — a different surname (Beckley) and person a century later.

A St Vincent administrators' roster naming George Berkeley — almost certainly the same man (the entry gives lt.-gov. of St Vincent 1864) but the roster carries no position/honour, so with a single initial it cannot be auto-confirmed. Review.
A 1948-50 roster entry — too late for a man who retired in 1881. Review/likely artifact.

notes: production high-value slice batch 4, hand-adjudicated (Claude Code, in-session)
